page.title: Marbella Boat Rental for Wedding Reception: Venue Guide & Package Ideas

data.meta_description: Plan a marbella boat rental wedding reception with our venue guide. Charter from €749/2h for up to 12 guests. Book your floating celebration today.
data.summary: Discover how to plan a marbella boat rental wedding reception with our fleet guide. From intimate gatherings to lavish parties, find the perfect vessel for your special day.
key_facts: Fleet includes 27 boats from 6.4m to 29m, all departing Puerto Banús
Tier A boats from €749/2h up to €2,299/8h, all inclusive
Tier B Mangusta 80 from €4,719/4h with free jet ski
Every charter includes licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT
Maximum capacity 12 guests on most yachts, 11 on Azimut 39, 10 on Pershing 46
All depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's premier marina
Suitable for ceremonies, receptions, or civil partnerships at sea

faq.q: Can we hold a wedding ceremony on a boat in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, civil ceremonies are possible on most of our yachts. You'll need to arrange a legal officiant separately. The captain can anchor at scenic spots like Cala del Faro or off the Golden Mile for a backdrop of La Concha mountain. Maximum guest numbers vary by boat, so check capacity when booking.

faq.q: What is included in the wedding reception package?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). For larger receptions, you can bring your own catering or arrange a private chef—just confirm with us beforehand. Tier B boats also include a free jet ski for added fun.

faq.q: How many guests can we invite for a wedding reception?
faq.a: Most of our yachts accommodate up to 12 guests, including the Mangusta 80, Azimut 58 Flybridge, and Ferretti 94. The Azimut 39 holds 11, and the Pershing 46 holds 10. For smaller parties, the Speedboat or Mariah SX21 take up to 6 guests. Always confirm final numbers at booking.

faq.q: What is the best boat for a wedding reception in Marbella?
faq.a: For a luxurious reception, the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 guests) offers ample deck space and a flybridge. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 guests) is a popular choice with its sleek design and included jet ski. For a more intimate affair, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 are excellent value at €749/2h.

faq.q: Can we decorate the boat for the wedding?
faq.a: Yes, you can bring decorations such as flowers, balloons, and banners. However, we ask that you avoid anything that could damage the boat or interfere with navigation. Confirm your plans with our team in advance to ensure everything is safe and permitted.

faq.q: How long is a typical wedding reception charter?
faq.a: Most wedding receptions are booked for 4 to 8 hours, giving you time for a cruise along the coast, a stop for photos, and a celebration at anchor. Minimum rental is 2 hours on Tier A boats, while Tier B boats require a minimum of 4 hours. You can extend the charter on request.

faq.q: Where will the boat depart from for the reception?
faq.a: All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's iconic marina. From there, you can cruise towards Estepona, Cabopino, or Cala Cortés. The captain will recommend the best route based on weather and your preferences, ensuring stunning views of the Costa del Sol.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the wedding day?
faq.a: Your safety is our priority. If adverse weather is forecast, we'll work with you to reschedule the charter or offer a full refund. The skipper will make the final call on the day. We recommend booking a backup date, especially for spring and autumn weddings.

data.body_html:
Imagine exchanging vows with the Mediterranean Sea as your backdrop, followed by a reception on the deck of a luxury yacht. A marbella boat rental wedding reception offers an unforgettable setting for your special day. Whether you're planning an intimate gathering or a lavish party, our fleet of 27 boats departing from Puerto Banús provides the perfect venue. From €749 for a two-hour charter to all-inclusive packages with a skipper and drinks, we make your wedding celebration seamless and spectacular.

Why Choose a Boat for Your Wedding Reception in Marbella

Marbella's coastline is a natural stage for a wedding. A boat rental gives you privacy, stunning views of La Concha mountain, and the freedom to anchor at exclusive spots like Cala del Faro or off the Golden Mile. Unlike a hotel ballroom, a yacht offers a dynamic setting where you can cruise, stop for photos, and dance under the stars. With all-inclusive pricing, you avoid hidden costs—just bring your decorations and enjoy.

Our Fleet: The Best Yachts for Your Celebration

Choose from a range of vessels, each departing from Puerto Banús. For a luxurious reception, the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 guests) offers expansive deck space and a flybridge. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 guests) is a favourite for its sleek lines and included jet ski—perfect for a fun photo op. For a more intimate affair, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 guests) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 guests) provide excellent value at €749/2h up to €2,299/8h. All boats include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.

Wedding Reception Package Ideas

We offer flexible packages to suit your vision. A two-hour sunset cruise (from €749) is ideal for a small ceremony and champagne toast. For a full reception, book a four-hour charter on a Mangusta 80 (from €4,719) with a free jet ski for entertainment. An eight-hour celebration on the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 guests) allows time for cruising to Estepona, anchoring at Cala Cortés, and a sit-down dinner. You can also bring your own catering or arrange a private chef—just let us know in advance.

What's Included: No Hidden Surprises

Every charter comes with a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). This means you can focus on your celebration without worrying about extra costs. For larger groups, we can accommodate additional catering or decorations. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80 variants) also include a free jet ski, adding a touch of adventure to your reception.

Planning Your Itinerary: Scenic Routes and Photo Spots

Departing from Puerto Banús, your captain can tailor the route to your preferences. Popular options include cruising towards Cabopino for its natural dunes, heading west to Estepona for a colourful old town backdrop, or anchoring at Cala del Faro for a secluded cove. The Golden Mile offers iconic views of Marbella's luxury hotels and La Concha mountain. For sunset photos, position the boat off the coast of Río Verde for golden-hour light.

How to Book Your Marbella Boat Rental Wedding Reception

Booking is simple. Explore our full fleet online and select your preferred yacht. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), you can book directly at fixed prices. For other vessels, contact us via WhatsApp for a personalised quote. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance, especially during peak season (June–September). Let us know the number of guests, desired duration, and any special requests—we'll handle the rest.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can we hold a wedding ceremony on a boat in Marbella?

Yes, civil ceremonies are possible on most of our yachts. You'll need to arrange a legal officiant separately. The captain can anchor at scenic spots like Cala del Faro or off the Golden Mile for a backdrop of La Concha mountain. Maximum guest numbers vary by boat, so check capacity when booking.

What is included in the wedding reception package?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). For larger receptions, you can bring your own catering or arrange a private chef—just confirm with us beforehand. Tier B boats also include a free jet ski for added fun.

How many guests can we invite for a wedding reception?

Most of our yachts accommodate up to 12 guests, including the Mangusta 80, Azimut 58 Flybridge, and Ferretti 94. The Azimut 39 holds 11, and the Pershing 46 holds 10. For smaller parties, the Speedboat or Mariah SX21 take up to 6 guests. Always confirm final numbers at booking.

What is the best boat for a wedding reception in Marbella?

For a luxurious reception, the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 guests) offers ample deck space and a flybridge. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 guests) is a popular choice with its sleek design and included jet ski. For a more intimate affair, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 are excellent value at €749/2h.

Can we decorate the boat for the wedding?

Yes, you can bring decorations such as flowers, balloons, and banners. However, we ask that you avoid anything that could damage the boat or interfere with navigation. Confirm your plans with our team in advance to ensure everything is safe and permitted.

How long is a typical wedding reception charter?

Most wedding receptions are booked for 4 to 8 hours, giving you time for a cruise along the coast, a stop for photos, and a celebration at anchor. Minimum rental is 2 hours on Tier A boats, while Tier B boats require a minimum of 4 hours. You can extend the charter on request.

Where will the boat depart from for the reception?

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's iconic marina. From there, you can cruise towards Estepona, Cabopino, or Cala Cortés. The captain will recommend the best route based on weather and your preferences, ensuring stunning views of the Costa del Sol.

What happens if the weather is bad on the wedding day?

Your safety is our priority. If adverse weather is forecast, we'll work with you to reschedule the charter or offer a full refund. The skipper will make the final call on the day. We recommend booking a backup date, especially for spring and autumn weddings.

Ready to start planning your dream wedding at sea? Browse our fleet of Marbella boat rentals and contact us to secure your date. For more inspiration, check out our guide to party boat charters or sunset cruises. Your perfect wedding reception awaits on the water.
